hero.title: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.
hero.subtitle: A living space for art, research, and collective imagination.

about.title: About
about.body: The Gihanga Institute of Contemporary Art (GICA) in Kigali is an independent non-profit art institute dedicated to contemporary art and to fostering critical exchange. Spanning 777 sqm, GICA was designed by Rwandan architect Amin Gafaranga and co-founded by Kami Gahiga and Kaneza Schaal. GICA encompasses a range of contemporary art practices including visual art, performance, film and literature. Through its professional art facilities, GICA is committed to providing artists in Rwanda, is credited in Rwandan mythology and oral traditions, technlogies, and practices that shaped Rwandan society.
about.mission: “ GICA' mission is to provide access to contemporary art for all Rwandans and to position the region within global art conversations. Our vision is to establish Rwanda as a leading hub for contemporary art and intellectual exchange “
about.gihanga: Gihanga is the mythical first king and founding father of Rwanda. He is celebrated for introducing the core cultural and technological advancements of early society, symbolizing the link between historical tradition and modern progress
about.symbol: The symbol inspired by the Grey Crowned Crane, represents the Great Lakes region and embodies artistic vision, awakening, and cultural unity.

contributors.title: Contributors
contributors.subtitle: We would like to thank the following individuals and organizations who have supported us along the way, which has helped us build and provide a solid foundation for artistic experiment

benefactor.heading: FOUNDING BENEFACTOR
benefactor.body: We are grateful for the generous support extended to us by the Mellon Foundation, our Founding Benefactor.
